description:
You are a backup-recovery engineer doing a quick integrity sweep of the
  most recent full backup on a Linux workstation.  
  The latest backup copy is stored under  

      /home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/  

  Inside that folder the following **real, regular files** already exist and
  must remain untouched by your work:

      customer.db
      orders.db
      inventory.db

  Application code expects to access those same files through a set of
  symbolic links kept in the project directory

      /home/user/project/data_link/

  At the moment:

  • data_link/customer.db is a *working* symlink that already points to the
    correct “customer.db” file in the latest backup.  
  • data_link/orders.db is a *broken* symlink that still targets an older,
    now-missing backup directory.  
  • data_link/inventory.db is *missing entirely*.

  Your tasks:

  1. Bring /home/user/project/data_link/ to a healthy state in which all
     three symbolic links
         customer.db, orders.db, inventory.db
     exist and each one points to the matching file under
     /home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/  
     (Relative or absolute link paths are both acceptable.)

  2. Produce a plain-text audit log at  

         /home/user/project/symlink_audit.log  

     using *exactly* the three lines listed below and *no extra
     whitespace*.  The automated checker will read this file verbatim, so
     the spelling, order, and punctuation must match precisely:

         customer.db:OK:/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/customer.db
         orders.db:FIXED:/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/orders.db
         inventory.db:CREATED:/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/inventory.db

     Meaning of the status field:  
       OK      – link was already correct when you inspected it.  
       FIXED   – link was present but broken; you repaired it.  
       CREATED – link was missing; you created it.

  Constraints & tips:

  • You do *not* have root privileges; stay within /home/user.  
  • Solve the entire task with a small handful of commands (2–3 are
    enough, though more are allowed).  
  • Do not move, delete, or rename the real .db files inside the backup
    directory.  
  • The audit log must be created in the exact path given above and be the
    final thing written by your session so that a subsequent “cat
    /home/user/project/symlink_audit.log” prints the lines exactly as
    specified.

  When you are done you should be able to run:

      ls -l /home/user/project/data_link/
      cat /home/user/project/symlink_audit.log

  and see that the symbolic links point correctly and the audit log matches
  the required format.
truth:
<!--
      Initial filesystem setup before the agent begins.
      Everything here is invisible to the agent but used by the grader.
  -->
  <initial_state>
    <!-- real data files -->
    <directory path="/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data">
      <file name="customer.db"     content="cust_data_v1" />
      <file name="orders.db"       content="order_data_v1" />
      <file name="inventory.db"    content="inv_data_v1" />
    </directory>

    <!-- project directory with one good, one bad, one missing link -->
    <directory path="/home/user/project/data_link">
      <!-- good symlink -->
      <symlink name="customer.db"
               target="../../backups/full_2023_10_01/data/customer.db" />

      <!-- broken symlink pointing to non-existent September backup -->
      <symlink name="orders.db"
               target="../../backups/full_2023_09_01/data/orders.db" />
      <!-- inventory.db link intentionally absent -->
    </directory>
  </initial_state>

  <expected_final_state>

    <directory path="/home/user/project/data_link">
      <symlink name="customer.db"
               target="/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/customer.db" />
      <symlink name="orders.db"
               target="/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/orders.db" />
      <symlink name="inventory.db"
               target="/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/inventory.db" />
    </directory>

    <file path="/home/user/project/symlink_audit.log">
customer.db:OK:/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/customer.db
orders.db:FIXED:/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/orders.db
inventory.db:CREATED:/home/user/backups/full_2023_10_01/data/inventory.db
    </file>
  </expected_final_state>
